title: Unique Woodfired Korean Cherry Bake
description: A fermented and aerated Korean dish featuring dried cherries, baked to perfection in a wood-fired oven, perfect for a crowd, this macrobiotic-friendly recipe is a unique twist on traditional Gyeongsang cuisine. It combines the natural sweetness of cherries with the savory flavors of Korean fermentation.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the dried cherries, korean chili flakes, and brown rice flour.
In a separate bowl, mix the fermented Korean chili paste with water to create a smooth paste.
Add the chili paste mixture to the dry ingredients and stir until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a warm place to rise for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
Preheat the wood-fired oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Punch down the dough and shape it into a round loaf.
Place the loaf in the wood-fired oven and bake for 30 minutes, or until it is golden brown.
Remove the loaf from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before slicing.
Drizzle the loaf with sesame oil and sprinkle with salt before serving.
